[2018-02-12 02:07] weiseguy: I think there are some minor inconsistencies with wording that should be cleaned up before the game launches
[2018-02-12 02:08] weiseguy: For instance, exhaust on wincer is referred to as "disabled until 3 turns from now" but on chrono filter it says "cannot do anything until 2 turns later"
[2018-02-12 02:10] weiseguy: Frost brooder has the symbols for buildtime and lifespan on it, but does not specify what they do (I think it is the only unit in the game that does not include reminder text for keyworded ability symbols)
[2018-02-12 02:11] weiseguy: I would change the definition of fragile from "can be damaged" to "can be partially damaged" since, well, everything in the game can be damaged if you have enough attack.
[2018-02-12 02:13] weiseguy: And I wonder if the distinction between "sacrifice" (killing itself) and "consume" (killing another friendly unit) is worth having 2 different words for paying a very similar cost
[2018-02-12 02:13] weiseguy: obviously these changes are all somewhat aimed at having greater clarity for new players

[2018-02-12 02:16] weiseguy: I mean damage literally is the thing killing non-fragile units (aside from apollo/deadeye)
[2018-02-12 02:16] crash_overlord: damage and damaged arent the same thing

[2018-02-13 01:07] elyot: actually let me explain a bit
[2018-02-13 01:07] elyot: we hired this team to do media and influencer outreach
[2018-02-13 01:07] elyot: they offered to run our social media and I was like "we don't need that"
[2018-02-13 01:08] elyot: and they were like 'nah we have a full-time social media person, they can run it all for you and post twice a day"
[2018-02-13 01:08] elyot: and I was like OK let's try it
[2018-02-13 01:08] elyot: Guess the verdict is in...
[2018-02-13 01:09] elyot: This has nothing to do with anybody needing more time... PR team wanted more time so that the media/press people would have more days with the game to get a feel for it and write their articles before launch
[2018-02-13 01:09] elyot: these tweets were probably all put together today
[2018-02-13 01:10] mrguy888: Maybe they should have asked for more time so they could have a few days with the game too. :Lul:
[2018-02-13 01:10] elyot: as far as I know, most of their team played the game, like we actually gave them keys and looked in the logs to make sure they redeemed them and played games
[2018-02-13 01:10] elyot: cuz we didn't wanna sign with a PR team who didn't give a shit about the game
[2018-02-13 01:10] elyot: one of their guys gave me extremely detailed and helpful feedback on the campaign
[2018-02-13 01:11] elyot: obviously he was not the social media person
[2018-02-13 01:11] crash_overlord: Each memeber played 5minutes while an intern had to play on 10 accounts to seem as if they care :POGGERS:
[2018-02-13 01:12] gex__ (gex): "memeber" is now the new member 😜
[2018-02-13 01:12] elyot: and generally when I have conversations with these people, they seem to at least understand the game and what it's about. Like their PR person was very helpful with messaging for copy on the steam page
[2018-02-13 01:13] crash_overlord: That was intentional btw :redmag3Man:
[2018-02-13 01:13] elyot: I think the social media person was having a very bad day, but I'm a bit concerned that they didn't have any review process in place to catch this
[2018-02-13 01:14] elyot: I told them not a single post goes out without me reviewing it
[2018-02-13 01:15] elyot: anyways, I gotta get back to work, I'm making these cool little award graphics for our steam page
[2018-02-13 01:15] elyot: to make us look all accomplished and successful
[2018-02-13 01:16] 307th: keep up the good work! really looking forward to release
[2018-02-13 01:19] elyot: honestly I've never been more determined to make this thing succeed as much as possible
[2018-02-13 01:19] elyot: feels like final stretch but it's been a brutal onslaught of responsibilities and tasks

[2018-02-15 04:02] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Is there somewhere a breakdown of how a high-level or "perfect" game with just the base set should go? I feel that would help me understand how other units affect my gameplan.
[2018-02-15 04:04] redrame: I think that a lot of people have attempted such a thing, but most of the breakdowns had holes in them that were found later, and the majority of units change the game significantly in base+1
[2018-02-15 04:04] redrame: let alone base+5 or base+8
[2018-02-15 04:05] redrame: one old theory was that player 2 wins out with DD/DA but I think that one got poked out a while ago
[2018-02-15 04:06] redrame: I've never played a base set game of prismata in my life as far as I can remember and I don't feel I've suffered for it
[2018-02-15 04:07] iminabearsuit: Yeah, the main reason base set isn't considered 100% solved, is because no one really cares that much.
[2018-02-15 04:08] iminabearsuit: It doesn't really have an impact on games.
[2018-02-15 04:08] iminabearsuit: However, Arkanishu streams a lot right now, so if you want to learn a game from a top level player you should watch him live - or any of his vods over at https://www.twitch.tv/arkanishu92
Arkanishu92 - Twitch
Join the Golden-Cup discord: https://discord.gg/fdPWwt3

[2018-02-15 04:14] redrame: one thing that is definitely true about base set is that wall only absorb creates pretty small games
[2018-02-15 04:15] redrame: anything larger than p1's turn 3 animus build does laughably poorly
[2018-02-15 04:15] redrame: be careful with that too since advanced units can slow sets even if theyre not absorb
[2018-02-15 04:15] redrame: like aegis and  plexo cell for example
[2018-02-15 04:18] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): I feel with some Base + 5 sets that those cards shouldn't affect the game much for the first 5 turns or so, and it would be better to have a fundamental understanding of that kind of "base set"  standard opening.
[2018-02-15 04:18] ussgordoncaptain: actually even the weak cards impact the game a lot
[2018-02-15 04:19] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): But maybe thats just my inexperience.
[2018-02-15 04:19] ussgordoncaptain: because any reasonable green attacker dominates the red attack
[2018-02-15 04:19] ussgordoncaptain: Tarsier SUCKS, it's just the best BSO atttacker
[2018-02-15 04:19] redrame: funny you mention that since that's why most experienced players prefer base +8, but yeah for base +5 it does matter significantly more
[2018-02-15 04:19] redrame: also I'd give tarsier about a C+
[2018-02-15 04:20] redrame: splitter C gauss cannon C- outside breachproof
[2018-02-15 04:20] ussgordoncaptain: There's some weird shenanigans you can pull with 1 tarsier lines
[2018-02-15 04:20] redrame: that's of course without any context
[2018-02-15 04:20] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Like I swear I've had some sets where it was 5 mid to late game units
[2018-02-15 04:20] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): and i feel like i had already lost the game before either myself or my opponent played any of them
[2018-02-15 04:20] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): because i didn't understand the base set opening
[2018-02-15 04:21] redrame: do you have a replay?
[2018-02-15 04:21] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): is there ever a time when there are 5 late-game units in your set and none of them are blockers, so you just ignore them?
[2018-02-15 04:21] redrame: replay analysis is probably a lot better for that
[2018-02-15 04:21] redrame: that's not unheard of
[2018-02-15 04:22] redrame: it largely depends which ones
[2018-02-15 04:22] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): no replay right now, i was just thinking about Prismata while walking to work haha
[2018-02-15 04:22] redrame: you can usually push any unit out if you really have to
[2018-02-15 04:22] redrame: but there's a cost if it's large and your opponent goes low econ tarsier rush
[2018-02-15 04:23] redrame: sometimes you have to do pretty stupid looking things to get the unit out though
[2018-02-15 04:23] redrame: if your opponent is rushing you you can't always afford to do hyperefficient openings
[2018-02-15 04:24] redrame: like for example in base set + odin the correct strategy against base set gameplay is still to buy odin
[2018-02-15 04:24] redrame: but you probably dont want to get 20 drones or your opp will already have a massive army by then
[2018-02-15 04:25] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): So you have to bank a bunch of gold just to get Odin?
[2018-02-15 04:25] redrame: not necessarily a bunch, but at least a couple gold
[2018-02-15 04:25] redrame: I dont remember the exact game
[2018-02-15 04:25] redrame: it's something you learn to get a bit of a feel for though
[2018-02-15 04:26] redrame: rule of thumb is that expensive legendary units beat base set as long as they hit the board
[2018-02-15 04:28] supergreyman (Greyman): Elyot's analysis of Base+Odin game, if you're interested:
http://blog.prismata.net/2015/06/10/balancing-prismata-openings-part-3/
Balancing Prismata Openings Through Unit Design – part 3: Variable Rush Timings - Prismata Blog
Hi everyone! Continuing from where we left off last time, we’re going to talk about one of my favourite Prismata topics—rush timings. Every Prismata player has been in a situation where they’ve been caught off guard and lost to their opponent’s early Redeemer, Shadowfangs, or Tia Thurnax. But when do these rushes work, when do …
[2018-02-15 04:28] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Oh nice, I definitely will read that.
[2018-02-15 04:28] redrame: forgot about that one
[2018-02-15 04:28] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): one more question. I don't even know how to begin to analyze sets with really out-there units
[2018-02-15 04:29] supergreyman (Greyman): You should actually read part 1-4 of that article series. Really good stuff.
[2018-02-15 04:29] redrame: this article isnt particularly detailed to that question though
[2018-02-15 04:29] redrame: it's much more about odin vs odin
[2018-02-15 04:30] redrame: since it assumes both people know to go odin
[2018-02-15 04:31] redrame: I also can't say I've ever done turn 8 odin 😛
[2018-02-15 04:31] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, the moment your opponent diverges into something else that isn't Odin, you better have Plan B.
[2018-02-15 04:32] supergreyman (Greyman): Which will happen like.... >50% of the chance.
[2018-02-15 04:32] redrame: the other thing to note is that there absolutely are expensive units that are meant to utilize support from other advanced units
[2018-02-15 04:32] redrame: a big sign that a unit is one of those is an unusual tech cost
[2018-02-15 04:33] redrame: for example, apollo is a very slow unit that requires you to grab 3x blastforge
[2018-02-15 04:33] redrame: in base set you'd have no way to utilize that and apollo just doesnt do enough on its own
[2018-02-15 04:33] redrame: not every unit is an odin

[2018-02-15 04:33] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): so if it was base + apollo only, you would just ignore apollo?
[2018-02-15 04:33] redrame: against base set you want smorc numbers and absorb numbers
[2018-02-15 04:34] supergreyman (Greyman): I heard it's been proven that you'll lose from going for Apollo, in Base+Apollo only.
[2018-02-15 04:34] redrame: shadowfang is also (I think) a non-unit against base set
[2018-02-15 04:34] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): is it just too slow?
[2018-02-15 04:35] redrame: it's not that you can't buy them
[2018-02-15 04:35] iminabearsuit: Shadowfang is very slow.
[2018-02-15 04:35] redrame: it's that you can't buy enough of them for the added efficiency to be worth it
[2018-02-15 04:35] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): i mean, too slow to effectively buy against base-set, which is fast
[2018-02-15 04:35] redrame: because any turn not buying shadowfang is a turn where the animus is dead
[2018-02-15 04:35] iminabearsuit: You require an additional 6 gold investment before you get damage. So it doesn't pay off until you reach like 7-8 attack. Which is way more than base set erquires.
[2018-02-15 04:35] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Right, so you're better of just getting Tarsiers
[2018-02-15 04:36] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): since they'll be effective faster, don't require as much investment, etc
[2018-02-15 04:36] redrame: yeah, that's where attackers get really complicated
[2018-02-15 04:36] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): and maybe there's no window to get shadowfang out effectively
[2018-02-15 04:36] iminabearsuit: Shadowfange becomes a lot stronger once there's good defensive units, and you require to get higher attack numbers to break throuugh
[2018-02-15 04:36] redrame: I have shadowfang rated as a b compared to c+ from tarsier
[2018-02-15 04:36] redrame: but in reality shadowfang is much more situational but higher potential
[2018-02-15 04:37] redrame: if you see a unit like nitrocybe that can make use of the 2nd animus in a pinch, you're a lot more inclined to buy shadowfang for example
[2018-02-15 04:38] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Alright one more question. Oxide Mixer seems really good because it threatens a lot of damage and forces a lot of defense.
[2018-02-15 04:38] supergreyman (Greyman): Now you're learning how to read set. 😄
[2018-02-15 04:39] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): But I didn't realize it and played against someone who got them early and I felt like I had my back pressed against the wall, but I ended up winning anyway.
[2018-02-15 04:39] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Did they just seem threatening, but actually were not?
[2018-02-15 04:39] redrame: It depends a lot on the situation
[2018-02-15 04:39] apooche: Oxide mixer is the best unit in the game
[2018-02-15 04:39] redrame: oxide is a decent unit but nothing to write home about
[2018-02-15 04:39] apooche: Whoever buys more typically wins
[2018-02-15 04:39] iminabearsuit: Not really, but I wanted a third opinion that's contradictory to everyone elses.
[2018-02-15 04:40] redrame: yeah hope that's helpful 😛
[2018-02-15 04:40] iminabearsuit: It's good**** but with a bunch of astrixes.
[2018-02-15 04:40] redrame: anyway with oxide I usually don't think of when I want to buy it but rather when I don't want to buy something else
[2018-02-15 04:40] supergreyman (Greyman): Secret to beating Apooche: make 4 blastforges and buy out all the oxide mixers.
[2018-02-15 04:40] redrame: the fact that it's non-permanent makes it hard to build a core strategy around
[2018-02-15 04:41] redrame: but if you have a blastforge and don't need a wall
[2018-02-15 04:41] redrame: you're much happier to have oxide to buy than steelsplitter
[2018-02-15 04:41] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): But it's a permanent threat of damage
[2018-02-15 04:41] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): until you use it, right?
[2018-02-15 04:41] apooche: It will strike fear in the heart of your enemies
[2018-02-15 04:41] iminabearsuit: Tarsier is a permament source of damage.
[2018-02-15 04:41] mrguy888: Oxide is just an amazing value pixie. You buy it to not buy a splitter or waste a blue which is very common especially early.
[2018-02-15 04:42] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Maybe I was just overestimating how much it changed my gameplan
[2018-02-15 04:42] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): When my opponent was threatening a bunch of pixies
[2018-02-15 04:42] redrame: I think a useful comparison is oxide vs tarsier
[2018-02-15 04:43] apooche: A useful comparison is oxide vs 4 engineers
[2018-02-15 04:43] redrame: doing that does make oxide look worse than it is though
[2018-02-15 04:43] iminabearsuit: Oxide makes you buy 4 engineers. A tarsier makes you buy an engineer for the rest of the game. 🤷
[2018-02-15 04:43] apooche: Tarsier makes you buy a wall
[2018-02-15 04:43] iminabearsuit: With a bunch of asterisks attached
[2018-02-15 04:44] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Hmm alright. I'm starting  to get why this game is waaay deeper than I already thought haha
[2018-02-15 04:45] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Even without the legendary units

[2018-02-15 04:45] iminabearsuit: Just wait until you get into Inflation. That's where the real magic happens.
[2018-02-15 04:45] redrame: if you're being extremely generous you can get that a tarsier costs 1/3rd of a gold more than oxide to attack for the remaining turns after the first four
[2018-02-15 04:45] supergreyman (Greyman): I gave up trying to learn about inflation.
[2018-02-15 04:46] redrame: that might help explain why oxide mixer isn't considered a primary attacker
[2018-02-15 04:46] apooche: But sometimes oxide does 2 damage
[2018-02-15 04:46] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): Oh ok, that makes sense.
[2018-02-15 04:46] redrame: as I said, primary attacker
[2018-02-15 04:46] redrame: oxide mixer is a great utility unit
[2018-02-15 04:47] redrame: you can exploit granularity and it's good value if the blue is otherwise dead
[2018-02-15 04:47] apooche: Give a man a pixie, and he will get an exploit this turn. Teach a man to make pixies, and he'll get exploits for life
[2018-02-15 04:47] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): So let's say my opponent spends a turn making a Shadowfang and I don't need to re-buy a wall
[2018-02-15 04:47] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): I can buy an Oxide the next turn instead?
[2018-02-15 04:48] redrame: I'm not sure why the scenario is necessary but yes
[2018-02-15 04:48] redrame: the important part of that scenario is "I don't need to re-buy a wall"
[2018-02-15 04:48] mrguy888: Shadowfang is the worst unit to make that example with because with shadowfang it is usually no but with most other attackers it is yes.
[2018-02-15 04:49] redrame: it's still yes with shadowfang
[2018-02-15 04:49] apooche: Oxide mixer is slow, but vs fang you gotta go 💨
[2018-02-15 04:49] redrame: unless you're floating 3 gold to buy it
